title: Vertical Sleeve Gastrectomy Protocol
author: Kristin Evans
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.za3f2gn

steps.title: Animals should be fasted overnight to...
steps.instruction: Animals should be fasted overnight to ensure that their stomach and intestines are as empty as possible.

steps.title: Weight prior to surgery to calculate...
steps.instruction: Weight prior to surgery to calculate drugs (Meloxicam and Baytril dosing)

steps.title: Autoclave all supplies and tools needed...
steps.instruction: Autoclave all supplies and tools needed for the surgery, start the bead sterilizer for sterilizing instruments.

steps.title: Prepare sterile field surrounding the microscope and surgisuite surgical platform
steps.instruction: Prepare sterile field surrounding the microscope and surgisuite surgical platform. Turn on warming field.

steps.title: Prepare sterilized supplies onto the sterile...
steps.instruction: Prepare sterilized supplies onto the sterile field that will be needed during the surgery.

steps.title: Anesthetize mouse using isoflurane induction chamber 5% to effect
steps.instruction: Anesthetize mouse using isoflurane induction chamber 5% to effect. Once animal is unconscious they may be moved to the prep area and maintained under 1-1.5% isoflurane. Artificial tear salve is placed on each cornea to protect from drying.

steps.title: Prepare the animal by clipping the...
steps.instruction: Prepare the animal by clipping the hair from the surgical site (The length of the areas is from the knee to the armpit area and the width should cover the entire ventrum).

steps.title: Perform a surgical scrub on the...
steps.instruction: Perform a surgical scrub on the incision area using an initial wipe of Betadine surgical scrub followed by 70% alcohol working from the intended incision line outward. Continue preparing the surgical incision site by doing three successive scrub of Betadine solution followed by 70% alcohol using clean cotton tipped applicator for each application.

steps.title: Administer Meloxicam SR 2 mg/kg subcutaneously...
steps.instruction: Administer Meloxicam SR 2 mg/kg subcutaneously in the rear dorsal area.

steps.title: Spray the incision area using betadine...
steps.instruction: Spray the incision area using betadine solution before transferring the animal to the surgical area.

steps.title: Surgeon will then wash and dry...
steps.instruction: Surgeon will then wash and dry hands using soap and water and don sterile surgical gloves.

steps.title: Make small vertical midline skin incision...
steps.instruction: Make small vertical midline skin incision from just below the xiphoid process the mid abdomen at the height of the top of the hip.

steps.title: Cut along the linea alba from...
steps.instruction: Cut along the linea alba from the xyphoid to just below the umbilical region

steps.title: Gently using your gloved fingers or...
steps.instruction: Gently using your gloved fingers or sterile cotton tip applicator, pull the intestine away to expose the stomach

steps.title: Using a dry sterile cotton tip...
steps.instruction: Using a dry sterile cotton tip applicator gently place the applicator under the median and left liver lobes and under the stomach to elevate the stomach through the incision

steps.title: Using a sterile cotton tip applicator...
steps.instruction: Using a sterile cotton tip applicator bluntly dissect away the ligamentous fat from the stomach and tuck it back into the abdomen freeing all connections between the stomach and the liver

steps.title: Remove the ligament to the liver...
steps.instruction: Remove the ligament to the liver that sits in the lesser curvature of the stomach using small iris scissors

steps.title: Remove the ligament connecting the fundus...
steps.instruction: Remove the ligament connecting the fundus to the body wall using small iris scissors

steps.title: Place a cotton tip applicator under...
steps.instruction: Place a cotton tip applicator under the fundus to elevate the vascular gastrosplenic ligament connection that attaches to the fundus.

steps.title: Once the vascular connection is clearly...
steps.instruction: Once the vascular connection is clearly exposed use the electrocautery to fuse the vascular connection and free the stomach Vertical Sleeve Gastrectomy

steps.title: Once the stomach is isolated, moisten...
steps.instruction: Once the stomach is isolated, moisten the stomach and intestine with sterile saline using the gavage needle

steps.title: Be careful not to pull the...
steps.instruction: Be careful not to pull the stomach out of the body cavity as this can injure the vagus nerves located here. Be careful of the pancreas attachment to the pyloric portion of the stomach. Avoid manipulation of this area.

steps.title: The objective of the surgery is...
steps.instruction: The objective of the surgery is to remove 75-80% of the stomach

steps.title: Place the isolated stomach on a...
steps.instruction: Place the isolated stomach on a 2 x 2 gauze and irrigate with sterile saline to prevent drying

steps.title: Make an incision using the micro-scissors...
steps.instruction: Make an incision using the micro-scissors along the avascular seam of the greater curvature of the stomach from the right of the esophagus to where the pancreatic vessels attach to the stomach.

steps.title: Using a cotton tip applicator gently...
steps.instruction: Using a cotton tip applicator gently remove the stomach contents from the stomach incision onto the gauze

steps.title: Replace the gauze and flush the...
steps.instruction: Replace the gauze and flush the stomach area with sterile saline

steps.title: Replace the gauze with a fresh wet piece of gauze
steps.instruction: Replace the gauze with a fresh wet piece of gauze

steps.title: Using sterile 7 French Polyethylene tubing,...
steps.instruction: Using sterile 7 French Polyethylene tubing, size the gastric pouch remnant from the esophagus to the pylorus

steps.title: Using 7-0 PDS suture close the...
steps.instruction: Using 7-0 PDS suture close the stomach at the edge of the sizing with the 6 French tubing using a simple continuous suture through both layers of stomach wall creating a diagonal line from the right of the esophagus to the connecting vessels of the pancreas. This leaves approximately 1/3 of the stomach cranial to the suture line.

steps.title: Keep the remaining length of suture...
steps.instruction: Keep the remaining length of suture attached to close the stomach after removal of stomach tissue

steps.title: Using micro scissors, cut away the...
steps.instruction: Using micro scissors, cut away the portion of stomach tissue distal to the suture line being careful not to cut any of the previously laid suture material

steps.title: Closure of the stomach is done...
steps.instruction: Closure of the stomach is done using 7-0 PDS in a continuous mattress pattern moving from the pancreatic vessels to the esophagus.

steps.title: Additional single interrupted suture are used...
steps.instruction: Additional single interrupted suture are used if any gap areas are noted

steps.title: The remaining stomach pouch should have...
steps.instruction: The remaining stomach pouch should have a lumen diameter sized to 6 French and approximating 20-25% of the original stomach area SHAM Vertical Sleeve Gastrectomy

steps.title: Once the stomach is isolated, the...
steps.instruction: Once the stomach is isolated, the stomach will have a gentle pressure applied using a pair of hemostats at a location that is approximately 2/3 cranial to the greater curvature.

steps.title: The location of the clamp can...
steps.instruction: The location of the clamp can be gauged by using a piece of 6 French tubing as a sizing indicator externally from the esophagus to the pylorus

steps.title: Just below the gentle pressure line...
steps.instruction: Just below the gentle pressure line a two separate pieces of 7-0 suture will be run through the outer layer of stomach wall on both sides of the stomach Surgical Closure

steps.title: Perform an abdominal lavage with at...
steps.instruction: Perform an abdominal lavage with at least 25 ml of sterile saline using a gavage needle for at least 4 gavages (total 100 ml)

steps.title: Change the gauze under the stomach...
steps.instruction: Change the gauze under the stomach before doing the lavage and with each successive lavage to prevent stomach contents on the gauze from entering the abdomen

steps.title: Place the stomach back into the abdomen
steps.instruction: Place the stomach back into the abdomen

steps.title: Suction remaining saline using sterile cotton tip applicators and gauze
steps.instruction: Suction remaining saline using sterile cotton tip applicators and gauze

steps.title: Instill Baytril (20 mg/kg) diluted in...
steps.instruction: Instill Baytril (20 mg/kg) diluted in sterile saline into the abdominal cavity before closing

steps.title: Close the linea alba abdominal wall...
steps.instruction: Close the linea alba abdominal wall with 6-0 PDS in a simple continuous pattern

steps.title: Close the skin with 6-0 PDS...
steps.instruction: Close the skin with 6-0 PDS in a simple continuous pattern

steps.title: Use tissue glue over the suture line Post-operative care
steps.instruction: Use tissue glue over the suture line Post-operative care

steps.title: Animals will be provided with slurry...
steps.instruction: Animals will be provided with slurry diet for 3 days post-operatively consisting of the powdered original diet or test diet indicated for the animals study combined with water (1:1 ratio). The slurry will be replaced daily.

steps.title: Each animal will be observed once...
steps.instruction: Each animal will be observed once to twice daily for the first three days and then daily thereafter for 10 days (total of 14 of monitoring)

steps.title: Animals will be assessed for pain...
steps.instruction: Animals will be assessed for pain level, incision status, status of eating, fecal production, and hydration status.

steps.title: For the first three days following...
steps.instruction: For the first three days following surgery subcutaneous saline (1 ml) will be administered to all surgical animals.

steps.title: Animals will be transitioned onto regular...
steps.instruction: Animals will be transitioned onto regular chow pellets of the designated diet as indicated by the study at Day 3 post-surgery. From this point forward all animals will being pair feeding between CSG and pair fed Sham controls until shipping or study indicates

steps.title: Animal body weights will be collected...
steps.instruction: Animal body weights will be collected prior to surgery on the day of surgery, at 3 days post-operative, at 10 days post-operative, and at 14 days post-operative right before shipping or placement onto study.

steps.title: All post-operative observation records, body weight...
steps.instruction: All post-operative observation records, body weight records, and pair feeding information will be sent along with any shipped animal cohorts
